title: Air Fryer Garlic Parmesan Asparagus
description: Crispy, tender asparagus spears with a savoury garlic parmesan crust. High-volume, low-calorie side that takes under 15 minutes start to finish.
ingredients: 1 bunch asparagus (about 450g / 1 lb), woody ends snapped off
instructions: Preheat your air fryer to 400°F for 3 minutes.
Snap the woody ends off 1 bunch of asparagus (about 450g). Pat completely dry with paper towel — moisture is the enemy of crispiness.
In a large bowl, toss the asparagus with 1 tsp extra virgin olive oil, 2 cloves minced garlic, 1/4 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp black pepper, and 1/4 tsp garlic powder until evenly coated.

Arrange spears in a single layer in the air fryer basket. Don't pile them — cook in two batches if needed. Air fry at 400°F for 7–9 minutes, shaking the basket at the halfway point. Thinner spears will be done at 7 minutes; thicker ones closer to 9.
Pull the basket and immediately sprinkle 2 tbsp grated parmesan over the hot spears so it melts slightly.
Finish with a squeeze of 1 tbsp lemon juice. Serve immediately.
notes: Asparagus is 94% water — dry it well or it steams instead of crisps. Single layer is non-negotiable in the air fryer. For extra crunch, a light mist of canola spray before cooking helps. This is a side dish, not a protein source — pair it with your chicken breast to hit your numbers. At 85 cal and 6g protein per serving it earns its plate space without costing you anything meaningful.
